steps.title: Recognize the linear growth model
steps.content: The equation $0.6t + 7 = 11$ follows the structure: (starting value) + (rate) × (time) = (ending value). In any linear equation, the coefficient that multiplies the time variable is the rate of change. Here, 0.6 multiplies $t$, so 0.6 is the rate.

steps.title: Determine what 0.6 represents
steps.content: Hair grows from 7 inches to 11 inches, and $t$ is measured in months. The term $0.6t$ must yield inches of growth. For dimensional consistency: (0.6 inches/month) × ($t$ months) = inches. Therefore, 0.6 is the growth rate in inches per month. That matches choice B — B is correct.
